ShortDescription.Value: Ships confirmed office fixes plus refined software diagnostics, import fallbacks, and optional telemetry.

Publish.LongDescription:
No Office Demand Fix currently ships confirmed office-demand fixes plus a narrower experimental software troubleshooting path in Cities: Skylines II.
- Signature Phantom Vacancy fix: removes stale PropertyOnMarket and PropertyToBeOnMarket state from occupied Signature office and industrial properties so false free-space counts stop suppressing demand. - Office AI chunk-iteration hotfix: one low-stock office no longer stops later offices in the same chunk from consuming stock and queuing virtual exports. - Office-demand baseline direct patch: restores the older `2x` office resource-demand baseline instead of the newer vanilla `3x` multiplier so evidence remains comparable to earlier runs. - Software import seller fallback: a narrow outside-connection seller correction that appends additional active outside connections for office virtual-resource imports when vanilla seller selection is too restrictive. - Software import buyer fallback: a narrow corrective pass for zero-weight office inputs that remain below threshold without a buyer, path, or trip, while skipping offices that already have same-resource current trading. - Diagnostics: optional office-demand and software-troubleshooting logs now expose demand-recovery counters, seller-resolution detail, and buyer-state probes so troubleshooting can distinguish recovered demand from software-fulfillment latency. Disabled by default until you turn them on. - Performance telemetry: optional coarse, render-aligned summary and stall CSV output for before/after comparisons; disabled by default until you turn it on.
Current release status:
- fixes the reproduced Signature phantom vacancy case - fixes the vanilla office AI chunk-iteration abort on low stock - restores the pre-hotfix office-demand baseline for comparability via a direct Harmony patch - includes refined experimental software import seller and buyer fallbacks by default, with richer diagnostics available but disabled by default - keeps optional render-aligned performance telemetry available for before/after comparisons - keeps broader software-related office stalls under investigation across demand recovery and software-fulfillment latency - keeps non-signature phantom vacancy under observation
Publish.ChangeLog: - Added explicit comparison-status and queue-sampling health fields to the optional performance telemetry path for current validation runs. - Expanded optional software troubleshooting diagnostics with demand-recovery, seller-resolution, and buyer-state probes so logs can separate office-demand recovery from software-fulfillment latency. - Refined the experimental outside-connection seller and virtual office buyer fallbacks, including skipping corrective buyers when same-resource current trading already exists. - Updated troubleshooting docs and raw-log tooling for the richer software diagnostics fields used by current investigations.
